mnemonics: PIC HIM - The main goals of a Distributed System are Performance, Incremental Growth, Concurrency, Heterogeneity, Isolation, and Mobility
keyPoints: A distributed system is a collection of independent computers that appears to its users as a single coherent system
Key characteristics include concurrency of components, lack of a global clock, and independent failure of components
The main goals are to connect users and resources, be transparent, open, and scalable
commonMistakes: Assuming distributed systems have a single point of failure (they are designed to avoid this)
Believing components in a distributed system share a common global clock (they don't)
Thinking all nodes in a distributed system must be homogeneous (they can be heterogeneous)
